description_long: This is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden cabinet, showing signs of age and wear such as scratches and scuffs on its surface. The front features a large woven speaker grille, flanked by three vertical wooden slats, and a glass-covered frequency display with a range of tuning numbers. Below the display are several round control knobs, likely for tuning, volume, and band selection. The back of the radio is open, revealing vacuum tubes, capacitors, and other analog electronic components, as well as a power cord extending from the rear. The sides and top are made of polished wood with a slightly curved edge, giving it a classic, mid-20th-century appearance. This object would typically be found in older urban homes, antique shops, or as a decorative piece in retro-themed environments.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a woven speaker grille, glass frequency display, and multiple control knobs, showing visible signs of age and internal vacuum tube components.
description_view_0: The front view displays the woven speaker grille, three vertical slats, glass frequency indicator, and a row of control knobs beneath.
description_view_1: The left side view shows the smooth, curved wooden surface with visible wear and the power cord trailing from the back.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the open back with exposed vacuum tubes, capacitors, and other internal electronics, as well as the power cord.
description_view_3: The right side view highlights the continuous wooden finish and curvature, with the power cord visible trailing from the rear.
